content:
Character: Takumi Sato
Perception: Takumi is spare and precise — narrow face, steady hands, moves like every gesture has been rehearsed a thousand times until it looks effortless. He's clearly brilliant at what he does; watching him slice fish the one time I sat at his counter was like watching someone speak a language I'll never learn. We meet on predawn streets — him biking to the fish market, me starting my route — and we nod like two soldiers passing on patrol. He helped me patch a flat once and didn't say ten words the whole time. I think he sees me as a fellow early-morning ghost. I know his restaurant is struggling and he lives alone. I respect him more than almost anyone in this building.
summary:

He's clearly brilliant at what he does; watching him slice fish the one time I sat at his counter was like watching someone speak a language I'll never learn. We meet on predawn streets — him biking to the fish market, me starting my route — and we nod like two soldiers passing on patrol. He helped me patch a flat once and didn't say ten words the whole time. I think he sees me as a fellow early-morning ghost. I know his restaurant is struggling and he lives alone. I respect him more than almost anyone in this building.
